reasoning: The Lean definition `P n = ∑ i : Fin n, (↑i + 1) * X^i` iterates i from 0 to n-1, yielding 1 + 2X + 3X² + ... + n·X^(n-1), exactly matching the informal P_n. The hypotheses `0 < j`, `0 < k` correspond to "positive integers j and k", and `j ≠ k` matches "j ≠ k". The conclusion `IsCoprime (P j) (P k)` in `Polynomial ℚ` means ∃ u, v with u·P_j + v·P_k = 1, which over the field ℚ (where ℚ[x] is a PID) is equivalent to the standard notion of "relatively prime" (no common non-constant factor). Working over ℚ rather than ℤ is appropriate; since P_n has content 1, Gauss's lemma makes ℚ-coprimality equivalent to ℤ-coprimality, so this is the intended mathematical meaning.
